uk: Свв. сімох отроків, що в Ефесі
saints.en: Sts. Seven Youths of Ephesus. Ven. Protomartyr Eudocia

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» 6 стихир: 3 отроків., 3 прпмчц., Сл., І нині: богород. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар отроків., Сл.: прпмчц., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря прпмчц. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. отроків. і прпмчц.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар отроків. (2), Сл.: прпмчц., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря прпмчц. Катизми чергові, сідальні Октоїха. Канон Октоїха, отроків. і прпмчц. На 9-ій пісні – «Чеснішу від херувимів». По 9-ій – світильний Октоїха, отроків., Сл.: прпмчц., І нині: богород. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар отроків., Сл.: прпмчц., І нині: богород. за гласом тропаря прпмчц. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. отроків. і прпмчц.
Часи: на 1-му – тропар дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му – отроків. та 9-му – прпмчц.
Літургія: вся Служба – дня. Ап. – 2 Кор. 172 зач.; 2, 14 – 3, 3. Єв. – Мт. 95 зач.; 23, 23-28.

troparion_en.text: Your holy martyrs, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received their incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion_en.text: Those who renounced the perishing comforts of this world, / preferring the eternal things of Heaven, / were incorrupt after death and rose from the dead / and buried the snares of the devils! / O Faithful, let us then honor them, singing a hymn of praise to Christ!

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received their incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text: Those who renounced the perishing comforts of this world, / preferring the eternal things of Heaven, / were incorrupt after death and rose from the dead / and buried the snares of the devils! / O Faithful, let us then honor them, singing a hymn of praise to Christ!

troparion.text: Your lamb Eudokia calls out to You, O Jesus, in a loud voice: / "I love You, my Bridegroom, and in seeking You I endure suffering. / In baptism I was crucified so that I might reign in You, / and I died so that I might live with You. / Accept me as a pure sacrifice, / for I have offered myself in love." / Through her prayers save our souls, since You are merciful.

Your holy martyr, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Eleutherius;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.
